source: Generated from a per-system Morphous manifest and prompt records. CSS variables are shadcn/tweakcn-compatible.
typography: Inter or Geist for compact incident operations UI with Noto Sans JP guidance for Japanese headings, body, labels, and tabular alert data.
layout: Dense shadcn/tweakcn grids, 8px radii, angular ice-chip dividers, frosted alert panels, restrained storm-cloud texture, and precise status surfaces for severe-weather monitoring.

prompts.label: Transparent Hailstone motif source
prompts.prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: transparent catalog motif source for Morphous design system, later background removal
Primary request: a single hailstone ice motif for a weather design-system bundle named Hailstone
Scene/backdrop: perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for background removal; one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation
Subject: one large faceted hailstone, irregular rounded ice pellet, translucent white ice body, frosted edges, slate-cloud internal inclusions, pale blue refractions, sharp crystalline chips and tiny impact pitting; looks like storm cloud hailstone ice, not a snowflake or gemstone
Composition: centered isolated object, generous padding on all sides, full subject visible, no cast shadow, no contact shadow, no reflection
Style: polished high-resolution 3D product cutout with realistic ice translucency but clean silhouette for masking
Palette: white ice, near-white frost, slate cloud gray, pale storm blue, faint cyan highlights; do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject
Avoid: text, watermark, logo, labels, multiple stones, background scenery, snowflake shape, generic blue gemstone, floor plane

prompts.label: Light-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous catalog design-system board, light mode, final board should be 3840x2160 or 4K-class 16:9
Primary request: Create a comprehensive light-mode design-system board for slug morphous-hailstone, name Hailstone, category weather, context storm cloud, motif hailstone ice, system feel incident alert system.
Visual direction: crisp operational interface for severe weather incidents and infrastructure alerts, grounded in hailstone ice: translucent whites, icy blue refractions, slate storm cloud panels, sharp crystalline borders, frosted glass overlays, impact-pit texture accents. Avoid generic blue-purple gradients and avoid decorative blobs.
Layout: one polished 16:9 board with clear large headings and legible labels. Include sections for palette, typography, multilingual type guidance for English and Japanese, spacing/radius/shadow tokens, components, navigation, dashboard/data, forms, texture, icon style, and sample usage. Keep labels large enough to read in preview.
Required UI coverage: buttons, inputs, selects, textarea, checkboxes, radio, switch, tabs, badges, incident alerts, status banners, cards, tables, command/search, side navigation, top navigation, dashboard charts, timeline, empty state, error state, loading state.
Palette names: Ice White, Frost Veil, Hail Core, Storm Slate, Cloudline, Warning Blue, Impact Cyan, Deep Cell, Signal Red. Use mostly white ice, slate cloud, and blue with sparse red only for destructive incident states.
Typography: compact incident-console hierarchy, Inter-like Latin plus Noto Sans JP-like Japanese guidance, dense but readable operations UI.
Composition: include a small motif specimen panel showing a faceted hailstone cutout; use angular ice-chip dividers and subtle storm-cloud texture; no nested cards inside cards.
Text policy: use short labels only, no paragraphs, no marketing copy.
Avoid: illegible microtext, unrelated saturated colors, purple gradients, beige/brown palette, stock SaaS hero layout, watermark, logo, fake brand names.

prompts.label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompts.prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous catalog design-system board, dark mode, final board should be 3840x2160 or 4K-class 16:9
Primary request: Create a comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for slug morphous-hailstone, name Hailstone, category weather, context storm cloud, motif hailstone ice, system feel incident alert system. It must feel like the same system translated from the light board into dark mode.
Visual direction: severe weather incident operations interface, dark storm-cell surfaces, black-slate command panels, translucent hail ice highlights, icy blue focus states, cloud-gray borders, frost-white text, sparse signal red for destructive alerts. Use angular ice-chip separators, frosted-glass overlays, impact-pit texture, and crisp data density. Avoid generic neon cyberpunk and avoid purple gradients.
Layout: one polished 16:9 board with clear large headings and legible labels. Include sections for palette, typography, multilingual type guidance for English and Japanese, spacing/radius/shadow tokens, components, navigation, dashboard/data, forms, texture, icon style, and sample usage. Keep labels large enough to read in preview.
Required UI coverage: buttons, inputs, selects, textarea, checkboxes, radio, switch, tabs, badges, incident alerts, warning banners, cards, tables, command/search, side navigation, top navigation, dashboard charts, incident timeline, empty state, error state, loading state.
Palette names: Black Ice, Night Cell, Deep Slate, Cloud Edge, Frost Text, Warning Blue, Impact Cyan, Hail White, Signal Red. Use mostly dark slate cloud, white ice, and blue with sparse red only for destructive incident states.
Typography: compact incident-console hierarchy, Inter-like Latin plus Noto Sans JP-like Japanese guidance, dense but readable operations UI.
Composition: include a small motif specimen panel showing a faceted hailstone cutout; use dark-mode equivalents of the same alert cards, data tables, search, forms, charting, and navigation patterns; no nested cards inside cards.
Text policy: use short labels only, no paragraphs, no marketing copy.
Avoid: illegible microtext, unrelated saturated colors, purple gradients, beige/brown palette, stock SaaS hero layout, watermark, logo, fake brand names.
